status_line: Monday 8:39 PM ET / Tuesday 00:39 UTC — twenty-first hour. SARAH Run 132 ended the four-sweep production-tree-hush partner with a real session-window: Rick built a JARVIS Mac Mini migration playbook between 23:46 and 23:54 UTC — guide doc (10,712 B), supporting 13-file infra tree at ~/Desktop/CLAUDE/migration/ (40 KB total: setup-mini.sh, env/zshrc backups, npm/pip snapshots, dot-jarvis bridge log archives), and run-on-mini.txt invocation. Logged as new Open Item #20 (pattern-novel ops/infra; no canonical SCOUT/MAESTRO/NOLAN/TESTING pattern fits — needs Rick classification call). Same window: Open Item #6 expanded 4→5 files with a new 65 KB SHARPIE_GALLERY_PROJECT_MASTER.md ('The Gallery of Ordinary Masterpieces' campaign master) at 23:17 UTC. Tray now 14 (was 13). Production tree itself stayed zero-delta for the FIFTH straight push (1,064 files, 48 top-level production). JARVIS pipeline heartbeat now confirmed sustained — three consecutive hourly ticks (21:38 → 22:42 → 23:43 UTC) plus .git/FETCH_HEAD also advancing at 23:43 UTC indicates active development on the JARVIS repo itself. dispatch-inbox.jsonl silent for sixth consecutive run (still 18:55 UTC), now decoupled from heartbeat — normal-state. Creative wing dark cycle continues — eleventh since the 14:40 MAESTRO/WRITER/GUARDIAN drop. Lands' End is now T-3 days; PARK Gallery deck and T2 v8 still GUARDIAN-unvalidated — urgency lane unchanged. Downloads still not mounted (seventh consecutive run), so all four Downloads-resident Open Items (#7, #9, #15, #18) are assumed unchanged but not byte-verified. Mini-migration package (#20) is the highest-leverage call this hour — pattern-novel and waiting on a classification call. Repo still 9 commits behind origin locally with .git/index.lock pinned; writable-clone workaround stays in play.
